sections.label: USER PROMPT
sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=21, col=39).
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 53, level 1.
Valid actions right now: MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current knowledge ledger from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE LEDGER.
Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `run_complete`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Knowledge ledger carried from earlier turns:
Game knowledge (persists across levels):
- Action semantics: {'charcoal_left_green_node10': 'Decrements green by one state (23→14→5) (VERIFIED steps 39-41)', 'charcoal_right_green_node12': 'Increments green by one state (5→14→23) (VERIFIED steps 39-41)', 'charcoal_top_yellow_node19': 'Decrements yellow 14→5 (VERIFIED step 35)', 'charcoal_bottom_yellow_node22': 'Increments yellow by one state (5→14→23) (VERIFIED steps 39-41)', 'dest_N_shape': 'Toggles green between adjacent states (VERIFIED steps 3-11)', 'dest_N_frame': 'Sets green to 23 (VERIFIED step 12)', 'dest_Y_shape': 'Toggles yellow between adjacent states (VERIFIED steps 5-6, 42)', 'red_dot': 'No gameplay effect (VERIFIED step 1)', 'red_cross': 'Static decoration, no effect (VERIFIED step 2)'}
- Object taxonomy: {'B': 'black background', 'G': 'dark gray (container frames, separators)', 'g': 'gray (outer container frames)', 'c': 'charcoal (inner boxes in destination containers - these are the real controls)', 'N': 'light green shapes (source node 2, dest nodes 13/14)', 'Y': 'yellow shapes (source node 16, dest nodes 20/23)', 'r': 'red dots: trackers in source containers (nodes 4,17) and red cross decorations (nodes 3,5,7,8,24,25,27,28)'}
- HUD map: {'row_63': 'gray/charcoal step counter bar, decreases by 1 pixel per action'}
- Win pattern: Unknown - tested states: (5,5), (14,5), (5,14), (23,14), (23,23), (5,23), (14,23), (14,14). None completed the level.
- Cross-level notes: Charcoal boxes in destination containers are increment/decrement controls for source shapes. Each source has 3 states: 5/14/23px.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it. Tag claims VERIFIED(step N) when the evidence is in `history`, otherwise HYPOTHESIS.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
The `world_model` tool argument is the reliable way to update the ledger. Assistant text is optional; if you write any, keep it short and prefix lines with `Action semantics:`, `Object taxonomy:`, `HUD map:`, `Win pattern:`, `Level log:`, `Cross-level notes:`, `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, or `Failed probes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 52 actions; a strong score needs about 20 or fewer. You are 2.6x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Let me analyze the current state more carefully. I have:
- Green source: 5 px
- Yellow source: 23 px
- Dest green shapes: 7 px each
- Dest yellow shapes: 7 px each
I've tried all 9 state combinations and none worked. The game over was triggered by clicking the green dest frame.
Let me think about what I haven't tried:
1. The G separator bars (nodes 11 and 21) - these are the dark gray bars between the charcoal boxes in each dest container
2. The yellow dest frame (node 18's outer frame) - but this might cause game over like the green one
3. The source container frames (nodes 1 and 15) - these are the dark gray bars next to source shapes
4. The black pixel inside the red cross (node 6 and 26)
5. The charcoal pixel at the bottom (node 30)
Let me try clicking on the separator bars first since they're part of the dest containers but I haven't tried them.
Actually, let me reconsider the problem. I've been assuming the goal is about state combinations, but maybe it's about something else entirely. Let me look at the structure:
Source containers:
- Green source: gray frame (G, 3px) + green shape (N) + red dot (r)
- Yellow source: gray frame (G, 3px) + yellow shape (Y) + red dot (r)
Dest containers:
- Green dest: gray frame (g, 36px) containing charcoal boxes (c, 18px each) with green shapes (N, 7px each), separated by dark gray bar (G, 5px)
- Yellow dest: gray frame (g, 36px) containing charcoal boxes (c, 18px each) with yellow shapes (Y, 7px each), separated by dark gray bar (G, 5px)
What if the goal is to click on the separator bars? Let me try that.
Actually, let me think about this differently. The dest containers have TWO charcoal boxes each. What if clicking on the separator between them does something different?
Let me also consider: what if the goal is to make the source shape match one of the dest shapes? The dest shapes are 7 px, and the source can be 5, 14, or 23 px. None match 7. Unless... the dest shapes can change too?
Wait, I just realized something. The dest shapes are 7 px each. What if they're supposed to be 7 px and the source shapes need to be something else? Or what if the goal is to make the source shapes disappear entirely?
Let me try a completely different approach: click on the G separator bar in the green dest container (node 11).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state: green=5, yellow=23. Need to try unclicked objects.
# Let me try the separator bars (G nodes 11 and 21) which I haven't clicked.

seg = current_frame.segmentation

# Find separator bars (G color, 5 pixels)
separators = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] == 5]
print("Separator bars:")
for s in separators:
    print(f"  id={s['id']}, boundary={s['boundary']}")

# Get center of each separator for clicking
for s in separators:
    boundary = s['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
    cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
    center_row = sum(rows) // len(rows)
    center_col = sum(cols) // len(cols)
    print(f"  Clicking separator id={s['id']} at ({center_row}, {center_col})")

# Try clicking the green dest separator (node 11)
# From boundary, get approximate center
sep11 = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['id'] == 11][0]
b = sep11['boundary']
r = sum(p[0] for p in b) // len(b)
c = sum(p[1] for p in b) // len(b)
print(f"\nClicking green separator at ({r}, {c})")
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c}])

# Check result
print("After click:")
print("  Game over:", last_action_result.get('game_over'))
print("  Level completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))
n_pixels = {n['id']: n['pixels'] for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] in ['N', 'Y']}
print(f"  N/Y pixels: {n_pixels}")
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"charcoal_left_green": "Decrements green (23->14->5)", "charcoal_right_green": "Increments green (5->14->23)", "charcoal_top_yellow": "Decrements yellow", "charcoal_bottom_yellow": "Increments yellow", "green_dest_frame": "Sets green to 23, triggered GAME_OVER", "dest_N_shape": "Toggles green between adjacent states", "dest_Y_shape": "Toggles yellow between adjacent states"}, "win_pattern": "Unknown - all 9 state combos failed", "cross_level_notes": "Game reset after game over", "level_log": "Level 1: GAME_OVER triggered, reset to green=5, yellow=23"}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "green=5, yellow=23. Game active.", "goal_model": "Trying separator bars as untested objects", "recent_findings": ["Separator bars (G, 5px) between charcoal boxes not yet clicked"], "open_questions": ["What do separator bars do?"], "current_plan": "Click green separator bar (node 11)", "strategy_log": ["State exploration exhausted", "Frame click caused game over"], "failed_probes": ["All 9 states", "red dots", "red crosses", "source shapes", "green dest frame"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
